tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses store Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: Japan's eyeglass prices can save you $400+ on designer frames — but which Kyoto shops carry what you need?
tasks.reward: Locks in early shopping plan so you can knock out all 4 pairs before temple hopping starts
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm arriving March 25 and want to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses early in the trip. I need stores that can do same-day or next-day service, accept foreign prescriptions, and ideally have English-speaking staff. Looking for a mix of budget-friendly chains (like Zoff, JINS) and shops with designer/unique frames. Give me a list with addresses, Google Maps links, price ranges, and turnaround times. Also note which area of Kyoto they're in so I can batch the shopping efficiently.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance: subject 'Japan eyeglass purchase coverage question'
tasks.hook_line: Can you hack your insurance to cover foreign eyeglass purchases and maximize the Japan price advantage?
tasks.reward: Clears the insurance question so you can buy confidently without surprise costs later
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out insurance and prescription requirements for buying prescription eyeglasses in Japan. I have US vision insurance and a current prescription. I want to buy 4 pairs during a trip to Japan (March 25 - April 7). What do I need to know about: (1) whether my insurance covers international purchases and how to submit claims, (2) whether Japanese opticians accept US prescriptions or need their own eye exam, (3) what documentation I need to bring or request for reimbursement. Give me specific steps to validate everything before I leave, including who to contact and what questions to ask.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps, search 'top temples Kyoto', pin 5
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up call?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo activities left
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto, Japan (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, bamboo forests, walking, outdoor spaces, quiet neighborhoods, and excellent food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, and suggested walking routes. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy neighborhood spots. Also recommend 2-3 restaurants per day with specific dishes to try. Optimize for avoiding crowds — we're okay with early mornings.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'best things to do Takayama Japan' and bookmark 3 articles
tasks.hook_line: Takayama's hidden in the Japanese Alps — fewer tourists, more sake breweries and mountain air.
tasks.reward: Fills in the Takayama gap so the entire trip route is planned
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 1.5 days in Takayama, Japan (March 27-28 evening, 2026). Coming from Kyoto, heading to Tokyo after. Takayama is a smaller mountain town in the Japanese Alps known for old streets, sake, and Hida beef. We like walking, local food, markets, and scenic areas. Give me a day-by-day breakdown with specific sites (Old Town, morning markets, any temples or shrines worth seeing), restaurant recommendations for Hida beef, and any day trips or hikes nearby if we have extra time. Include Google Maps links and realistic timing.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'Tokyo neighborhoods guide' and pick 3 to explore
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo has 23 wards and infinite rabbit holes — which neighborhoods match your vibe?
tasks.reward: Completes the entire Japan trip activity plan — everything locked in before departure
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 4 full days in Tokyo (March 29 - April 2, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like neighborhood walks, quirky shops, excellent food (sushi, ramen, izakaya), art/design, and people-watching. Not super interested in big tourist crowds. I want a day-by-day itinerary organized by neighborhood with specific places to eat, walk, and explore. Include a mix: one museum or art space, one big landmark (maybe teamLab or Meiji Shrine), and lots of wandering through cool neighborhoods like Shimokitazawa, Nakameguro, or Daikanyama. Also include fountain pen shopping (Tokyo limited edition Sailor pens). Give me Google Maps links and suggested routes.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open it in Cursor
tasks.hook_line: Developer portals are supposed to make life easier — can you crack the setup puzzle on this one?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all Cerebro work — you can finally dig into the actual project instead of being stuck at the door
nuggets: May need VPN or internal access
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally. This is a work project — an internal tool for engineers. I need to clone the repo, install dependencies, and get the dev server running. Walk me through: (1) what to check in the README or docs first, (2) common blockers for developer portals (Docker, databases, API keys, VPN requirements), (3) a step-by-step setup checklist. Assume I'm on macOS with Homebrew, Node, and Docker already installed. If there are multiple setup paths, recommend the fastest one for local development.

recommended_slots.reason: Outside work hours, trip is 6 days out — this low-effort research task fits the ramp_up energy and clears a quick win before the day starts
Low decision load coordination task — good for early morning before the brain fully turns on, and unblocks confident glasses shopping
Most important activity planning task with highest impact — Kyoto is 2.5 days and still blank, closes a major gap
